channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump makes special appearance at NASCAR's Daytona 500 and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration cuts hit FDA employees in food safety, medical devices and tobacco products
item.description: Nearly half of the FDA’s $6.9 billion budget comes from fees paid by companies the agency regulates, including drug and medical device makers, which allows the agency to hire extra scientists to swiftly review products. Eliminating those positions will not reduce government spending.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republicans hope to jam through defense, border funding boost ahead of shutdown fight
item.description: Senate Republicans have the ambitious goal of jamming through funding boosts for defense and border security — separately from the appropriations process — ahead of a mid-March deadline to prevent a government shutdown. Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Shaq's OGs win the 1st NBA All-Star mini-tournament, topping Chuck's Global Stars 41-25 in the final
item.description: An All-Star Game like none other — since it was three games, not one — still had plenty of flash and not a lot of fire. And in the end, Boston's Jayson Tatum had the dunk that decided a crown.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: At least 9 people are dead, including 8 in Kentucky, in latest blast of winter weather
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — At least nine people have died in the most recent round of harsh weather to pummel the U.S., including eight people in Kentucky who died as creeks swelled from heavy rain and water covered roads. Kentucky Gov.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration cuts reach FDA employees in food safety, medical devices and tobacco products
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration’s effort to slash the size of the federal workforce reached the Food and Drug Administration this weekend, as recently hired employees who review the safety of food ingredients, medical devices and other products were fired.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW PERRONE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald's teases 'new' chicken item, return of Snack Wraps in 2025
item.description: We knew Snack Wraps were returning to McDonald's this year, but now it looks like another item we've seen before is coming back.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Seven more massage therapists accuse Ravens' Tucker of sexually inappropriate conduct: report
item.description: A total of 16 massage therapists from eight Baltimore-area spas have now shared accounts with the Baltimore Banner.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Transgender man found dead in New York field endured repeated acts torture: police
item.description: “This is one of the most horrific crimes I've ever investigated,” Captain Kelly Swift of the New York State Police Bureau of Criminal Investigation said.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, ABC 4

item.title: Food delivery driver helped stop suspect following deadly Austria stabbing: officials
item.description: A 23-year-old with possible connections to the Islamic State group is in custody after a stabbing of six passersby that left a 14-year-old boy dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trans man found dead in New York field endured repeated acts torture, police say
item.description: A transgender man reported missing by his family last week endured repeated acts of violence before he was found dead in a field in upstate New York, according to state police.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Some in Hurricane Helene-ravaged North Carolina embrace Trump's push to abolish FEMA
item.description: President Donald Trump's suggestion about “getting rid of” the nation's disaster relief agency doesn't sound like a bad idea for some residents of western North Carolina who are still navigating the long recovery process from Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Investigators enter a highway tunnel in Wyoming where a fiery crash killed 2
item.description: Wyoming authorities have entered a highway tunnel to begin picking through the wreckage of a fiery multivehicle crash a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Here are the agencies purging federal workers
item.description: The moves come as President Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by tech billionaire Elon Musk, have exacerbated their efforts to cut down on government spending — in some cases looking to dismantle whole agencies or departments.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The new NBA All-Star format is here, and Sunday night will show whether it works
item.description: This will be an actual All-Star matchup. On one side, there's a team with 86 combined All-Star selections, $2.7 billion in on-court earnings and where every single player on the team is either an NBA champion, an Olympic gold medalist, or both.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: First homes and weddings have never been more expensive
item.description: Weddings and homeownership are two major goals for many couples, but affording both takes years of careful saving and planning.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Getler, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect in Tupac Shakur killing seeks to delay trial as defense identifies new witnesses
item.description: Attorneys for the man suspected of killing rap icon Tupac Shakur want to delay next month’s trial, saying more time is needed for investigative work to ensure that Duane “Keffe D” Davis gets a fair trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 20% down? The myth that could be holding home buyers back
item.description: Scraping together a down payment can be challenging in today’s housing market, but lower down payment can leave you with more on hand.
item.dc:creator: Rosie Cima, NewsNation

item.title: IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season, according to two sources familiar with the agency's plans, and cuts could happen as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wreckage of missing plane found in Florida woods
item.description: A plane that had gone missing Friday night was found early Saturday morning in a remote, wooded area in north Florida.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: U.S. Army bans transgender people from enlisting
item.description: The new policies, effective immediately, could affect more than 10,000 active duty troops, per AP estimates.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: AP reporter and photographer barred from Air Force One over 'Gulf of Mexico' terminology dispute
item.description: The White House barred a credentialed Associated Press reporter and photographer from boarding the presidential airplane Friday for a weekend trip with Donald Trump, saying the news agency’s stance on how to refer to the Gulf of Mexico was to blame for the exclusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What does 'Wawa' even mean? The company offers up a hint
item.description: In certain communities along the East Coast, the mere mention of the name “Wawa” conjures up visions of piping-hot coffee, scratch-off lottery tickets and pre-made Sizzli breakfast sandwiches.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Back-to-back earthquakes jolt Los Angeles area within hours
item.description: An earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 3.5 rattled the L.A. metropolitan area early Saturday just hours after a 3.8-magnitude quake shook the Malibu area shortly before midnight Friday.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KRON4

item.title: As Trump administration reforms the EPA, cleanups of America's most toxic sites are uncertain
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Just over a mile from where Patricia Flores has lived for almost 20 years, a battery smelter plant spewed toxic elements into the environment for nearly a century.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Black Hawk pilots may have missed air traffic directive, flown on 'bad' altitude data before crash
item.description: It is likely the Black Hawk pilots “stepped on” the communication for less than a second by pressing the microphone key to communicate back to the control tower, she said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, ABC 4

item.title: Dozens of couples get hitched at snowy Colorado ski resort on Valentine's Day
item.description: LOVELAND SKI AREA, Colo. (AP) — There is nothing like dozens of couples saying “I do” simultaneously to warm the crisp, frigid air that grips the Colorado Rockies in the heart of winter.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS PEIPERT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Abortions to resume in Missouri after a judge blocks restrictions
item.description: Abortions are set to resume in Missouri after a judge blocked regulations that had restricted providers even after voters approved enshrining abortion rights into the state’s constitution.
item.dc:creator: SUMMER BALLENTINE and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Andrew Lester pleads guilty in shooting of unarmed Kansas City teen Ralph Yarl
item.description: Andrew Lester pleaded guilty to second-degree assault after shooting 16-year-old Ralph Yarl outside Kansas City, Missouri home in April 2023
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, Fox 5

item.title: Proposed bill looks to make President Trump’s birthday a federal holiday
item.description: Rep. Claudia Tenney (R-N.Y.) announced Friday a proposed bill, the Establishment Act, which seeks to make President Trump’s birthday and Flag Day a federal holiday in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, KRON4

item.title: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. confirmed as Trump's health secretary after close Senate vote
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as President Donald Trump’s health secretary, putting the prominent vaccine skeptic in control of $1.7 trillion in federal spending, vaccine recommendations and food safety as well as health insurance programs for roughly half the country.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Eastern storm cuts power to tens of thousands as California braces for flooding
item.description: Storms dumped heavy snow and freezing rain on a swath of the U.S. East from Kentucky to the nation's capital, causing hundreds of traffic accidents, knocking out power in places and threatening to flood waterways as temperatures began rising Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JOHN RABY, Associated Press, ABC 4
